ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Ane̱ŋ a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ a saŋ a bo̱ mò̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ kanye ko̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ mò̱ se̱ dee‑ro, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Yo̱sɛf, Deefid mò̱ ke̱nanagyi, fo̱ ma sa a Mariya ke̱taa baa fo̱ aye̱ waa fo̱ kufu, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ya sa mò̱ kame‑o.
translated: While he was thinking about this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because what is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.

text: Ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd a wu. Mò̱ a wu‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a be̱e̱ le̱e̱ Yo̱sɛf se̱ dee‑ro Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.
translated: King Herod died. After he died, the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.

ref: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
text: Ne̱ Yo̱sɛf a ko̱so̱ taa kayaagyi‑o na mò̱ nyi, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi yɔ Isireelii swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.
translated: Joseph stayed in Egypt, he and his father's family, and he lived a hundred and ten years.

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: “Mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ na mo̱nꞌ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o a tɔ‑rɔ.”
translated: If you do good to those who do good to you, and do not do what God says you should do, it is not good.

ref: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a ba mò̱ ase̱. Bo̱ko̱ a le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ, bo̱ko̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ abe̱gya ke̱maa se̱, ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ e̱maŋ pɛɛɛ ne̱ e̱ maa Yo̱o̱daŋ bo̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱.

text: Ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ bamo̱ asawu bo̱ be̱ya puri amo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ baa gya Yeesuu si bo̱ yii.

translated: Someone may say to you, ‘The chief does not want you, so he has rejected you.’

text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ taa mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke bamo̱, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ gbaa maa taa mo̱ne̱ lee a ɔ bo̱ ke mo̱ne̱.” Ako̱ŋ ke̱kra ase̱ŋ
translated: If someone does something bad to you, and you also do something bad to them, then you are just like the person who did bad to you.

translated: He went to school and when he returned he saw someone sitting under the tree and asked him, “Who are you waiting for under the tree?” The person replied, “I am waiting for someone who will come and give me food.” He asked, “Who will give you food here? text: Ayaabraagyi amo̱ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ mɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa se̱sɛ ne̱ o nu abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ atɔ tɔɔraasɛ, na katanne̱kpa ne̱ ka taare̱ a ka pe̱nna se̱sɛ‑ɔ, ya lwee mò̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ, amɔ aa waa ko̱kyɔ kyo̱ŋ abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ a bo̱ mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. translated: If you want to go quickly, go alone, if you want to go far, go together.

text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ kaye̱ bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ke̱tɔ e̱ ba. Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ e̱ ba a bo̱ bo̱ lee ase̱sɛ bɔye̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ atimaa‑o‑ro.
translated: If someone does something bad to you, don't do the same thing back to them. Forgive them so that God will also forgive you your sins.

text: Mo̱nꞌ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ a du Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro‑o. N gye̱ owure ko̱ ya dɛɛ kye̱na aaa? Ne̱ mò̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ de mò̱ ko̱kɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ na ɔ nya ŋu mò̱ aa bamo̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ atanne̱ ne̱ a bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ na ɔ kɔɔre̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ase̱.

translated: If you see a snake on the road in front of you, do not try to kill it, just leave it and continue on your way.

text: Amo̱se̱ ne̱ owure‑o a te̱e̱ ane̱ŋ a ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Kuŋurole̱ŋ wuye ke̱yaafɔre̱ e̱ gye̱ fo̱. Ko̱kɔ ne̱ fo̱ de mo̱‑ɔ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ bɛɛɛ? Amaa fo̱ a ko̱re̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ nyite fo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ a taa fo̱ ko̱kɔ amo̱ bo̱ ke fo̱.
translated: The woman and her husband went to the farm. When they arrived there, she said, ‘My husband, stay here on the farm and wait for me. What should I bring for you when I return? If you want something, tell me, and I will bring it for you.’

text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ma kine mò̱ ka ke̱tɔ ke̱maa se̱, amɔ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a kyɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ kamɛɛ fe̱raa. translated: Jesus said, “Get up, take your mat and go home.” Immediately he stood up in front of them, took what he had been lying on and went home praising God. Everyone was amazed and gave praise to God. They were filled with awe and said, “We have seen remarkable things today.”

text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ mbraa?” Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mbraa ne̱ ŋ yɛ, ‘Ɔko̱ ma mɔɔ mò̱ bɛɛko̱, ɔko̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ ywii, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ te̱ŋ mò̱ bɛɛko̱ kanɔ,

text: Amaa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ i kii nsii-amɛɛ, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ nsii-amɛɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ mɔ kii agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱.”

text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ kii se̱sɛ timaa lo̱we̱, amo̱ fe̱raa, nare̱ ya fe ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ de‑o, na fo̱ taa atanne̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ sa atiripo̱. Fo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa kapo̱tɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ ye̱ra so̱so̱ sa fo̱. Na fo̱ bo̱ gya mo̱ se̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ.”

text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱ siidii kudu na bo̱ yɔ ya suŋ sa mò̱ kake amo̱ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ baa sure si, ne̱ baa yɔ mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya suŋ.

text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, ‘Anyare̱ mɔ ne̱ baa ba fo̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ a suŋ kusuŋ dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ane̱ mɔ ane̱ a kpo̱ne̱ suŋ kusuŋ ibiri mɔ pɛɛɛ na kyɔwe̱ a da ane̱. Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa fo̱ a ka ane̱ aa bamo̱ atanne̱ ko̱ŋko̱ dɛɛ ne̱e̱.’
translated: He said, ‘A sick person does not go to the farm, but if he gets well, he will go and work with his friends. So if you are not well, stay at home until you are well. Don’t go to the farm and make your sickness worse.’
